comment.name: Comment
comment.text:
Morning Report


Last Updated: 3/15/26 | 6:26AM

Good afternoon, Sunday River!

The best way to wrap up a great weekend is with a few more afternoon laps across the eight peaks. Get them in while you can before lifts power down at 4PM.

After that, the grooming team will head out to smooth things over and clean up this weekend's turns.

Looking ahead, tomorrow is shaping up to be a wet one. Forecasts are calling for precip starting around 9AM and continuing through the day. That doesn't mean the skiing stops, though. Throw on the Gore-Tex, take advantage of the bubble lifts, and get ready to carve some super fun soft snow. It's just water anyway.

The rest of the week is looking much better. Sunshine returns, and thanks to the strong season we've had building up a solid base, one day of rain won't do much damage. There's a reason Sunday River is known for having the most dependable snow in New England.

Lastly, the time has come to renew your New England Pass for the 2026/27 season. If you don't already have one, you can still grab it at the lowest price of the year. As an added bonus, you can ski the rest of this season too. Either way, now's the time to buy and get the most bang for your buck.

We'll see you out there!

-Team Snow

comment.name: Comment 2
comment.text:
Good morning, Sunday River!

It's Sunday and lifts start spinning at 8AM. Once they do, 102 trails across the mountain will be open and ready to go, giving you plenty of terrain to roam once the chairs get moving.Conditions have been skiing really well lately, and yesterday's 4 inches of fresh snow gave the mountain a nice refresh. That new snow is still keeping things soft out there this morning, so surfaces should be feeling pretty nice right from the first .

Temps around the resort are starting at 17 at Locke Top, 19 at North Peak, 22 in Jordan Basin, and 27 down at the White Cap base. Highs this afternoon should climb to around 31 up top and 38 down low, with skies turning partly to mostly sunny.

The grooming team had a productive night and laid down fresh passes across the mountain, so early laps should be skiing smooth to start the day.

Friendly reminder: the 2026/27 New England Pass is now on sale. If you already have one, renewing now locks in the lowest price of the year. And if you didn't have a pass this season, you can still grab one now and ski the rest of this season as well.

And for passholders this weekend, retail shops across the resort are running up to 60% off, including Sunday River Sports, the Kuhl Store at the Summit, Aster & Birch, the Jordan Gift Shop, and Betty's Treats & Treasures.

Not a bad way to spend a Sunday.

We'll see you out there!

-Team Snow
